title: Add multi-project support with central core and per-project executors
description:
Transform kb from a single-project tool into a multi-project system where each project has its own board, tasks, agents, and executor instance, all reporting back to a central core.

## Architecture

### Central Core (`~/.pi/kb/kb-central.db`)
A new SQLite database acting as the system-wide hub:
- **Project registry** — name, working directory, status (active/paused/errored), isolation mode (in-process or child-process)
- **Unified activity feed** — aggregated task events (created, moved, merged, failed) across all projects with project attribution
- **Global concurrency limits** — a system-wide max concurrent agents cap that spans all projects, preventing resource exhaustion (e.g., max 4 agents total, not 4 per project)
- **Project health tracking** — per-project status indicators, in-flight task counts, last activity timestamp, agent uptime
- **Resource monitoring** — CPU, memory, and API usage per project, surfaced on the overview dashboard
- **Shared model/auth config** — model presets and auth credentials shared across projects (extends existing global settings pattern)

### Per-Project Runtime
Each registered project gets its own scoped set of engine components:
- `TaskStore` (pointing to the project's `.fusion/fusion.db`)
- `TaskExecutor`, `TriageProcessor`, `Scheduler`, `WorktreePool`, etc.
- All components scoped to the project's working directory
- Each executor reports events (task transitions, errors, resource usage) back to the central core

### Executor Lifecycle (Hybrid Model)
- **Default: in-process** — project engine components run as instances inside the main dashboard Node process. Projects are activated lazily when selected and can be suspended when idle.
- **Opt-in: child process isolation** — projects can be configured to run in a separate child process via IPC. One project crash doesn't affect others.
- Central core abstracts over both modes with a uniform `ProjectRuntime` interface so the dashboard/API doesn't care which mode a project uses.

## Dashboard UX

### Overview Page (Home)
- Shows all registered projects with health cards: status, active task count, last activity, resource usage
- Unified activity feed timeline across all projects
- Global concurrency usage indicator
- "Add Project" button triggers setup wizard

### Project Drill-Down
- Clicking a project enters the existing full board view, scoped to that project
- All existing task/board/settings views work as-is within a project context
- Breadcrumb or back-nav to return to the overview

### First Run Experience
- Interactive setup wizard when no projects are registered
- Prompts for project name, working directory, and basic configuration
- Auto-migration path: if user has an existing `.fusion/` directory, the wizard pre-fills the cwd and detects the existing board, requiring only a name to complete registration

## CLI Changes

### New `fn project` subcommands:
- `fn project list` — list all registered projects with status
- `fn project add [dir]` — register a new project (interactive wizard)
- `fn project remove <name>` — unregister a project (does not delete `.fusion/` data)
- `fn project info [name]` — show project details and health

### Existing commands:
- Auto-detect project from cwd (walk up to find `.fusion/` dir, match against registry)
- `--project <name>` flag on all task/settings commands to target a different project
- `fn dashboard` launches the multi-project dashboard with overview + drill-down

## Migration
- On first run post-upgrade, if a `.fusion/` directory exists in cwd but no central database exists, auto-create `~/.pi/kb/kb-central.db` and register the current project
- Wizard prompts for a display name (defaulting to directory name)
- Existing `.fusion/fusion.db` data is untouched — the central DB only holds registry metadata and cross-project state
- Fully backward-compatible: single-project users see minimal friction

log.outcome: Initial plan: Add the concept of projects in the tool. Each project has its own dashboard, tasks, agents, etc. A project has one working directory. A project can be added or removed. On first run a project should b

This is a well-structured specification for a major architectural transformation. The spec correctly identifies the existing single-project architecture (TaskStore per `.fusion/fusion.db`, Scheduler/TaskExecutor per project) and proposes a coherent multi-project model with a central core (`~/.pi/kb/kb-central.db`) managing multiple project runtimes.
